note: Second anchor map, needed because the two hosts render DIFFERENT Projects lists against the SAME design state. Design side is identical to anchors.json's `default` rows (app-v3 ProjectsSurface list). Live side is the chat-surface CardGrid that apps/desktop/renderer/destinationBinders.tsx:563-568 mounts — <ProjectsDestination items={result} onRetry={retry} /> with NO renderDetail/focusedProjectId, so the desktop host has no project-detail state to measure at all (that absence is reported in FINDINGS.md, not here). Kept out of anchors.json so the web report is not polluted with false missing-in-live rows: both files share the same design selectors, and one anchors file can carry only one `live` selector per label.

expectDivergence.color: Per-project hue is intentional (D3): live persists color_hue + ships a hue picker; the mock's !important tile neutralisation is a leftover, not intent. Recorded divergence.
expectDivergence.backgroundColor: Per-project hue is intentional (D3): live persists color_hue + ships a hue picker; the mock's !important tile neutralisation is a leftover, not intent. Recorded divergence.
expectDivergence.borderColor: Per-project hue is intentional (D3): live persists color_hue + ships a hue picker; the mock's !important tile neutralisation is a leftover, not intent. Recorded divergence.

expectDivergence.extra: Filter tabs are deliberate live chrome — the mock has no filter tabs (D4).
New-project control is deliberate live chrome — the mock ships no create affordance (D4).
